name: Hippocampus
location: Medial temporal lobe, forming floor of lateral ventricle's inferior horn
description: Seahorse-shaped archicortical structure fundamental for episodic memory consolidation, spatial navigation, and cognitive mapping.
functions: Spatial cognitive mapping via Place Cells and Grid Cells
Pattern separation and pattern completion in trisynaptic loop (DG-CA3-CA1)
Adult neurogenesis in dentate gyrus subgranular zone
bloodSupply: Posterior Cerebral Artery (PCA) & Anterior Choroidal Artery
clinicalImportance: First structure affected in Alzheimer's disease; bilateral resection causes total anterograde amnesia (Patient H.M.).
researchNotes: Nobel Prize winning discovery of place cells (O'Keefe) and grid cells (Moser) in hippocampal-entorhinal axis.
interestingFacts: London taxi drivers have significantly enlarged posterior hippocampi from learning 'The Knowledge' street map.
